,< 
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Dorothy Benker 
25501 Sugar Pine Drive 
Pioneer, California 95666 
Dear Mrs. Benker: 
September 30, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our file No. 88-345 
Your August 23, 1988, letter to Attorney General John 
Van de Kamp has been referred to the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
You have requested advice concerning your disclosure 
responsibilities under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1:1 
QUESTION 
Are you obligated to file a Statement of Economic Interests 
(Form 730) because of your position as a commissioner on the Amador 
County Airport Land Use commission? 
CONCLUSION 
The conflict of interest code for the Amador County Airport 
Land Use Commission is not yet in effect. 
commissioners will be 
designated in the conflict of interest code. 
Therefore, when the 
code becomes effective you will be required to file a statement of 
economic interests. 
FACTS 
The County of Amador is in the process of adopting a conflict 
of interest code for the Amador County Airport Land Use Commission 
which is wholly within the County of Amador. 
The conflict of 
interest code for the Amador County Airport Land Use Commission has 
not become effective yet. 
The Amador County Board of Supervisors is 
the code reviewing body for this commission. 
11 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


Dorothy Benker 
September 30, 1988 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
Each state and local government agency is required to submit a 
conflict of interest code for approval within six months after the 
agency comes into existence. 
(Section 87303.) 
The proposed 
conflict of interest code must be approved by the code reviewing 
body_ 
Review and approval of the code must be carried out under 
procedures which guarantee to officers, employees, members and 
consultants of the agency and to residents of the jurisdication 
adequate notice and a fair opportunity to present their views. 
(Section 87311.) 
Conflict of interest codes become effective 30 
days after approval. 
Initial Statements of Economic Interests (Form 
730) must be filed no later than 30 days after the effective date of 
the conflict of interest code. 
(Section 87302.) 
As a designated employee, you must file a statement of 
economic interests pursuant to the disclosure category for your 
position as set out in the conflict of interest code. 
On an 
"initial" statement, you must disclose investments and interests in 
real property of the type described in your disclosure category 
owned directly, indirectly or beneficially by you, your spouse or 
your dependent children if the interests are located in or doing 
business in your jurisdiction.£! 
I hope this has been helpful to you. 
If you have any 
additional questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662. 
By: 
AAH:ssa 
Very truly yours, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
&;£)rk~51~·Z~/ 
Alice Hughes 
Technical Assistance & 
Analysis Division 
£! 
"Jurisdiction" means the district or other geographical 
area in which the Amador county Airport Land Use Commission has 
jurisdiction. 
Real property is "within the jurisdiction" if the 
property is located in or within two miles of any land owned or used 
by the Commission. 


25501 Sugar Plne Drive 
Pioneer, CA 
95666 
August 23, 1988 
Attorney General John Van de Kamp 
1515 K Street, #511 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Sir: 
I am a retired private citizen serving as a Commissioner 
on the Amador County Airport Land Use Commission. 
Amador County Counsel provided Commissioners with State of 
California (FPPC) 1987-1988 Form 730 Statement of ~conomic 
Interests forms to be filled out. 
I consulted the Amador County Clerk for a Disclosure 
Category and was informed there is nothing in place in the 
County requiring my filing this form. 
• 
Ky economic interests are held jointly with my husband and 
he refuses disclosure of our holdings, none of which are in 
conflict with the airport. 
~y question is this. 
Is filing a Statement of Economic 
Interests a requirement of my serving as a Commissioner on 
the Airport Land Use Commission? 
I would appreciate an answer to my question as soon as 
possible, and I thank you very much for your assistance in 
this matter. 
Since~ely yours, 


JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP 
Attorney General 
State 0/ California 
DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE

deem appropriate.

Please 
contact this office if we can be of assistance to you.

reach me at (916) 324-5765 . 
Very truly yours, 
K. VAN DE KAMP

Dear Ms. Benker: 
September 6, 1988 
Re: 
88-345 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on August 31, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. 
Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

commissioners will be
